title: dryopteris costularis
intro: Dryopteris costularis, also known as the Costularis Wood Fern, is a species of fern native to East Asia, characterized by its lacy fronds and distinctive leaflets. The plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly for its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to relieve respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The health benefits of Dryopteris costularis include reducing inflammation, improving respiratory function, and acting as an antioxidant to protect against cell damage. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
